summary: Implemented ADR 0235 from the isolated latest-origin/main worktree by shipping the PatternFly-style cross-application launcher with session-scoped favorites and recents in the interactive ops portal, hardening the ops-portal runtime replay to assert the launcher partial, and live-applying the change to docker-runtime-lv3 through the documented ADR 0191 narrow in-place mutation exception after isolating competing branch-local portal replays.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml --with jsonschema --with fastapi==0.116.1 --with httpx==0.28.1 --with cryptography==45.0.6 --with PyJWT==2.10.1 --with jinja2==3.1.5 --with itsdangerous==2.2.0 --with python-multipart==0.0.20 pytest tests/test_interactive_ops_portal.py tests/test_ops_portal_runtime_role.py tests/test_ops_portal.py tests/test_runtime_assurance_scoreboard.py -q` returned `25 passed in 12.74s`, and `make syntax-check-ops-portal` passed after the runtime role began asserting the launcher partial and launcher copy during replay.

The first governed replay `make live-apply-service service=ops_portal env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true -e ops_portal_repo_root=/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.worktrees/ws-0235-live-apply'` failed closed before mutation and instructed the operator to use immutable replacement or rerun with `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true`; `make immutable-guest-replacement-plan service=ops_portal` confirmed the narrow exception rule for this governed `docker-runtime-lv3` service.

The next replay with `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` proved the defect was concurrent branch-local sync drift rather than missing launcher code: a competing `ops_portal` replay rewrote `/opt/ops-portal/service`, leaving `/partials/launcher` at `404`, `docker logs ops-portal` full of launcher 404s, and `/opt/ops-portal/service/ops_portal/app.py` at hash `ceb70927becdeab1a391c4b710bc1c094f4c9de24a94501151217ad8bbc43874` with no `/partials/launcher` route.

After obtaining an uncontended portal replay window, `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=ops_portal env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true -e ops_portal_repo_root=/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.worktrees/ws-0235-live-apply'` completed successfully with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 ok=126 changed=22 failed=0 skipped=17`, and the play passed `Verify the application launcher partial renders locally` plus `Assert the application launcher is present`.

`receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-29-adr-0235-live-hashes.txt` captured `/opt/ops-portal/service/ops_portal/app.py` at `cf6ddb19b3353f718b5933cb589d6a3d1c3ae7377ceda033ed0e29e4bcfcc7ba`, `/opt/ops-portal/service/search_fabric/__init__.py` at `d9094470c32e2f93c71ba5b3eb4f2df7737186ec4e9299adb660a83e0194e904`, `/opt/ops-portal/data/config/workflow-catalog.json` at `3e9131c2beda07a91e4a419a8d5d1729ff92749be840f4fedb718f100c6a34c9`, `/opt/ops-portal/data/config/persona-catalog.json` at `86819853d16693a72c2d5119690043b66f8db825d43ce4a5b6873bb52437033b`, and route proof `@app.get("/partials/launcher", response_class=HTMLResponse)` at line `1324`.

verification.check: Internal launcher interaction and public edge
verification.observed: Internal checks in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-29-adr-0235-internal-endpoints.txt` showed `/health`, `/`, `/partials/overview`, and `/partials/launcher` all returning `200`, while `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-29-adr-0235-launcher-session-check.txt` recorded `before_favorites=1`, `before_recents=0`, `favorite_response_contains_favorites=1`, `after_favorites=1`, `after_recents=1`, `after_keycloak_mentions=7`, and `redirect_code=303` for `service:keycloak`. Public-edge proof in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-29-adr-0235-public-edge-check.txt` confirmed `https://ops.example.com/health` returned `200` with `{"status":"ok"}` and `https://ops.example.com/` preserved the expected oauth sign-in redirect.

notes: This replay used `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` as the documented ADR 0191 narrow exception because `ops_portal` is still governed by immutable guest replacement on `docker-runtime-lv3`.

Branch-local `ops_portal` applies share `/opt/ops-portal/service` on the guest. Concurrent replays from different worktrees can clobber each other's uploaded tree even when their controller checkouts are isolated; use an uncontended replay window for live applies.

Protected mainline files remain intentionally untouched on this workstream branch. The follow-up merge-to-main step still needs `VERSION`, `changelog.md`, `RELEASE.md`, the next release note, `build/platform-manifest.json`, `versions/stack.yaml`, and the top-level `README.md` status summary.
